title: telemetry — compliance evidence
description: Combined compliance surface for the three signals. Traces + metrics leak less than logs (attribute values are usually structured identifiers, not free-form message bodies), so logs carry the heavier redaction obligation. All three signals ship through the same shared redactor pipeline configured under telemetry.redact.*.

controls.evidence: Shared redactor pipeline strips PII from span attributes AND log records BEFORE export. Rules: global key patterns (password, token, cookie, authorization, api_key, credit_card, cvv, ssn, private_key), regex value patterns (credit card, SSN, IBAN), class-level #[RedactedForLogs] for domain models. Signal-specific overrides are additive, not subtractive — redaction can only grow, never shrink.

Log records may reference the erased subject via workspace_id + causer_id. GDPR Art. 17 does NOT require retroactive log erasure when logs are used for security / audit purposes. Sink retention naturally purges. Legal basis documented.
Third-party observability backends (DataDog, Grafana Cloud, Honeycomb) are subprocessors. See subprocessors.json. Self-hosted collectors are not subprocessors.
Span attributes + log records include academorix.workspace_id + causer_id — count as processing records. Documented per subprocessor.
OTLP transport is TLS 1.2+. Log sink transport is HTTPS. Bearer-token / API-key auth via OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_HEADERS + sink-specific env vars. Credentials rotated quarterly.
OTLP endpoint + sink credentials rotated quarterly. Stored as secrets, not in code.
Read access to traces + logs gated by platform.telemetry.view permission at UI level. Sink-level access controlled by sink IAM (Grafana Cloud team perms, DataDog user roles).
Health probes on the pipeline itself — dropped-spans-ratio + dropped-records-ratio + exporter-reachable + sink-reachable + redaction-enabled all feed alerting.
Logs are the primary source for incident forensics. Retention windows meet the 90-day minimum for security-event correlation.
Regex redactor patterns strip anything matching card-number regex before write. PAN / CVV / expiry never touched (they don't reach our code — payment provider tokenises client-side).
